articles.title: Oil Company Breaks Rules Many Times
articles.summary: A company called Essar, which runs an oil refinery in Cheshire, England, has admitted breaking environmental rules almost 500 times. This happened between 2018 and 2020. Some of the times, they put too much cyanide, a harmful chemical, into a canal, river, and brook near their refinery. Cyanide can hurt animals and plants. The company said sorry and is working to fix the problems. They went to court because of these rules, and the Environment Agency, which protects nature, brought charges against them. The company claims there was no harm to wildlife, but breaking rules is serious to keep our environment safe.

articles.title: Gum Germs and Breast Cancer: A Surprising Link
articles.summary: Scientists found that a germ from gum disease can travel to the breast and make cancer grow faster. This germ, called Fusobacterium nucleatum, lives in the mouth and can move through the blood to breast tissue. In studies with mice and human cells, the germ caused damage to DNA and made cells act in ways that help cancer start and spread. People with certain genes, like BRCA1, might be more at risk because their cells can hold onto the germ longer. The researchers say taking care of your teeth by brushing and flossing might help lower the chance of getting breast cancer, but more studies are needed to be sure. This shows how oral health is connected to overall health.

articles.title: Taxpayers to Clean Up Big Illegal Rubbish Dumps
articles.summary: In England, three huge illegal rubbish dumps are going to be cleaned up, and taxpayers will pay for it. These dumps are in Wigan, Sheffield, and Lancashire, and together they have 48,000 tonnes of waste. Normally, the landowner pays to clean up illegal dumps on private land, but this time the government is helping. People living near the dumps are happy because they have problems like bad smells, rats, and flies. One person, Nicha Rowson, had to move her work from home because of the smell. She is glad but thinks it took too long to decide. The government has a plan to stop waste crime and will check the sites before cleaning them. Another big dump in Kidlington is already being cleaned for over £9 million. Some villagers are angry because a super big dump found by the BBC is not being cleaned yet.

articles.title: Junk Food Can Hurt Your Heart
articles.summary: A new study shows that eating too much ultra-processed food, like chips, frozen meals, and sugary drinks, can make you more likely to have a heart attack or stroke. People who ate more than nine servings a day had a 67% higher risk compared to those who ate about one serving. Each extra serving added over 5% more risk. The study looked at 6,814 adults in the U.S. and found that this risk was even higher for Black Americans. The lead doctor, Amier Haidar, says these foods should be eaten in moderation because they are linked to heart disease. The research used food questionnaires to track what people ate and grouped foods by how processed they are. Even after considering things like calories and diet quality, the risk stayed high, suggesting that the way food is made might be harmful on its own.

articles.title: Natural History Museum Becomes Top UK Tourist Spot
articles.summary: In 2025, the Natural History Museum in London became the most visited tourist attraction in the UK. It had over 7.1 million visitors, which is 13% more than in 2024. This made it number one for the first time, beating the British Museum, which was top for the last two years. Other popular places included Windsor Great Park, Tate Modern, and the National Gallery. The Association of Leading Visitor Attractions said that visits to many sites are going up, but they are still lower than before Covid-19. The director of the Natural History Museum said they are happy and work hard to give visitors a great day out. People love learning about nature and culture, even when times are tough with money. The British Museum might have a good year soon because it will show the Bayeux Tapestry.

articles.title: Belly Fat and Heart Health: A Simple Guide for Kids
articles.summary: Scientists have found that belly fat, the fat stored around your waist, can be bad for your heart. Even if you are not overweight, having a big belly might increase your chance of getting heart failure, which is when your heart doesn't work well. This is because belly fat can cause inflammation, which is like your body getting swollen and angry inside, and that can hurt your heart. The study looked at nearly 2,000 adults in Mississippi over many years and measured their waist size and inflammation levels. Doctors say that checking your waist size might help find people at risk earlier than just looking at weight. So, it's important to eat healthy and stay active to keep your belly and heart strong.
